bullet_points: Load the dataset and parse chemical formulas to identify metal-oxide precursors and carbonate products.
Compute total DFT energies per formula unit by multiplying formation_energy_per_atom by nsites.

Implement a matching algorithm to pair oxide precursors (MxOy) with carbonate products (MaCO3) based on metal identity, documenting the stoichiometric balancing logic used to ensure dimensional consistency.
Calculate the reaction energy ΔE = E(MaCO3) - E(MxOy) - E(CO2) using the reference energy for CO2 (-11.17 eV).
Apply the stability filter (energy_above_hull ≤ 0.1 eV/atom) to all participating phases.
Print a detailed summary table of the number of oxide-carbonate pairs found per metal element (Ca, Mg, Li, Na, K, Ba, Sr, Mn, Fe, Co, Ni, Cu, Zn, Al).
Save the resulting matched dataset of thermodynamically accessible reaction pairs to disk.
sub_tasks.sub_task: Reaction Space Mapping and Thermodynamic Filtering

bullet_points: Train a Random Forest regressor to predict S298 using structural (volume_A3, density_g_cm3, nsites, crystal_system) and composition features (nelements, formation_energy_per_atom, element fractions).
Print cross-validation R² and RMSE scores to quantify model reliability.
Calculate the reaction entropy ΔS = Sproducts - Sreactants - SCO2(gas), where SCO2(gas) = 213.8 J/mol·K.
Compute the regeneration temperature Treg = ΔH / ΔS (using ΔH ≈ ΔE).
Print full distribution statistics (mean, median, std, min, max, percentile counts) of Treg before and after the 300–600°C filter.
Filter for reactions with Treg in the 300–600°C range and ΔS < 0.
Save the updated dataset containing thermodynamic and regeneration metrics.
sub_tasks.sub_task: Entropy Estimation and Regeneration Temperature Calculation

bullet_points: Calculate the Volume Expansion Ratio (ΔV / Voxide) for each reaction pair.
Apply the electronic filter (band_gap > 0.5 eV) to exclude metallic phases.
Print a detailed breakdown of candidate counts eliminated at each filtering stage (hull, band gap, volume expansion, Pareto selection).
Identify the Pareto-optimal set of materials based on ΔE, Treg, and Volume Expansion Ratio.
Perform a sensitivity analysis by varying the CO2 reference energy by ±0.1 eV and print the number of candidates whose Pareto membership changes.

Generate a multi-panel high-resolution PNG containing: (a) ΔE vs. Treg scatter plot, (b) Volume Expansion Ratio box/violin plot by crystal system, (c) 3D Pareto-front scatter plot, and (d) bar chart of Pareto-optimal counts per metal element.
Save the final candidate list and Pareto-front data.
sub_tasks.sub_task: Structural Compatibility, Pareto Optimization, and Visualization

bullet_points: Interpret the thermodynamic landscape and identify the most promising elemental families for CO₂ capture.
Discuss the trade-offs between reaction favorability, regeneration temperature, and structural stability.
Evaluate the impact of the stability and electronic filters on the candidate pool.
Include a validation subsection cross-referencing computed ΔE and Treg for industrial benchmarks (CaO→CaCO₃ and MgO→MgCO₃) against literature values.
Synthesize the findings into a comprehensive report, referencing the generated plots and key statistics to validate the methodology.
